hero.title: Where Culture, Business and People Meet.

why.title: Why MaWePolo?
why.text: MaWePolo connects the Veneto region in Italy and the Małopolska region in Poland, two regions distant yet remarkably similar. Through this portal, we bring together exceptional opportunities for development and cooperation, bridging cultural, business, and tourism potential.

cities.title: Kraków
cities.text: The historic heart of Poland. Wawel Castle, the Main Square, Kazimierz, and centuries of royal heritage.

cities.title: Tarnów
cities.text: A charming city with Renaissance architecture, warm climate, and a beautifully preserved old town.

cities.title: Nowy Sącz
cities.text: A gateway to the mountains, wooden architecture, and rich regional traditions.

cities.title: Zakopane
cities.text: The winter capital of Poland. Tatra Mountains, wooden villas, and vibrant highlander culture.

cities.title: Oświęcim
cities.text: A city of memory and reflection, home to the Auschwitz‑Birkenau Memorial and Museum.

cities.title: Wieliczka
cities.text: Famous for its UNESCO‑listed Salt Mine — a world of underground chambers and salt sculptures.

cities.title: Bochnia
cities.text: One of the oldest cities in Poland, known for its historic salt mine and medieval heritage.

territory.title: Villages
territory.text: Lanckorona, Chochołów, Lipnica Murowana — wooden houses, folk traditions, and peaceful landscapes.

territory.title: Landscapes
territory.text: Tatra Mountains, Pieniny, Beskids, Dunajec Gorge — dramatic peaks, rivers, forests, and valleys.

gastronomy.title: Food & Beverages
gastronomy.text: Oscypek, kwaśnica, pierogi, regional cheeses, craft beers, and traditional highlander cuisine.

gastronomy.title: Products
gastronomy.text: Local crafts, wooden carvings, lacework, pottery, and regional specialties from mountain villages.

culture.title: Art
culture.text: Sacred art, wooden churches, folk costumes, and vibrant cultural traditions shaped by centuries.

culture.title: UNESCO Sites
culture.text: Kraków Old Town, Wieliczka Salt Mine, Kalwaria Zebrzydowska, Wooden Churches of Southern Małopolska.

economy.title: Craft
economy.text: Highlander craftsmanship, woodwork, textiles, and traditional artisan techniques passed down through generations.

economy.title: Businesses
economy.text: A dynamic region with tourism, technology, education, and local enterprises driving economic growth.

cities.title: Treviso
cities.text: A small gem crossed by canals and medieval arcades.

cities.title: Padua
cities.text: A historic university city with ancient traditions.

cities.title: Vicenza
cities.text: The city of Palladio, Renaissance elegance, UNESCO heritage.

cities.title: Belluno
cities.text: Gateway to the Dolomites, pristine nature and spectacular mountain landscapes.

cities.title: Rovigo
cities.text: Between the Po and Adige rivers, medieval history and the calm atmosphere of Polesine.

territory.title: Villages
territory.text: Asolo, Marostica, Bassano del Grappa: historic villages, medieval castles, and unique landscapes.

territory.title: Landscapes
territory.text: Dolomites, Lake Garda, Adriatic coast: enchanting mountains, lakes, hills, and plains.

gastronomy.title: Food & Beverages
gastronomy.text: Discover the soul of Italian cuisine — from traditional dishes to modern interpretations, all crafted with passion and the finest ingredients.

gastronomy.title: Products
gastronomy.text: Discover the unique products of Veneto — from traditional crafts to modern innovations.

culture.title: Art
culture.text: Discover the soul of Italian art — from Renaissance frescoes and sacred church interiors to timeless landscapes shaped by history, light, and beauty.

culture.title: UNESCO Sites
culture.text: Venezia, Padova, Dolomiti: world heritage sites.

economy.title: Craft
economy.text: Traditional Italian craftsmanship: ceramics, glassmaking, wood carving, textiles, and timeless artisan techniques passed down through generations.

economy.title: Businesses
economy.text: Local businesses: from industry and design to agriculture and craftsmanship, a diverse economy rooted in tradition and innovation.

krakow.description: Kraków is the cultural heart of Poland — a city of kings, scholars, artists, and centuries of history. Its old town, castles, and vibrant districts create a unique blend of tradition and modern life.

blocks.title: Wawel Castle
blocks.text: A symbol of Polish heritage, Wawel Castle overlooks the Vistula River and preserves royal chambers, cathedrals, and legends.

blocks.title: Main Market Square
blocks.text: One of Europe’s largest medieval squares, surrounded by cafés, churches, and the iconic Cloth Hall.

blocks.title: Kazimierz District
blocks.text: A historic Jewish quarter transformed into a lively area of cafés, galleries, and cultural events.

tarnow.description: Tarnów is a warm, elegant city with Renaissance architecture, charming streets, and a beautifully preserved old town.

blocks.title: Renaissance Old Town
blocks.text: Colorful façades, arcades, and a historic town hall create a unique southern-European atmosphere.

blocks.title: Cultural Heritage
blocks.text: Museums, churches, and monuments reflect the city’s multicultural past.

blocks.title: Sunny Climate
blocks.text: Tarnów is known as one of Poland’s warmest cities, ideal for walks and outdoor life.

nowy-sacz.description: Nowy Sącz is a gateway to the mountains, wooden architecture, and rich regional traditions.

blocks.title: Wooden Architecture
blocks.text: Open-air museums and historic churches preserve the region’s unique wooden craftsmanship.

blocks.title: Mountain Landscapes
blocks.text: The surrounding Beskids offer hiking, viewpoints, and peaceful nature.

blocks.title: Regional Traditions
blocks.text: Folk costumes, music, and cuisine reflect centuries of cultural heritage.

zakopane.description: Zakopane is the winter capital of Poland — a lively town at the foot of the Tatra Mountains, known for wooden villas, mountain culture, and outdoor adventures.

blocks.title: Tatra Mountains
blocks.text: Trails, peaks, lakes, and breathtaking views make the Tatras a paradise for hikers.

blocks.title: Highlander Culture
blocks.text: Wooden houses, music, and cuisine create a strong regional identity.

blocks.title: Krupówki Street
blocks.text: Shops, cafés, and local specialties bring life to the town center.

oswiecim.description: Oświęcim is a city of memory and reflection, home to the Auschwitz‑Birkenau Memorial and Museum.

blocks.title: Auschwitz‑Birkenau Memorial
blocks.text: A UNESCO site dedicated to remembrance, education, and historical truth.

blocks.title: City of Reflection
blocks.text: Oświęcim promotes dialogue, peace, and intercultural understanding.

wieliczka.description: Wieliczka is famous for its UNESCO‑listed Salt Mine — a world of underground chambers, sculptures, and saline lakes.

blocks.title: Salt Mine
blocks.text: A labyrinth of tunnels, chapels, and artworks carved entirely from salt.

blocks.title: Underground Lakes
blocks.text: Crystal-clear saline lakes create a magical atmosphere deep underground.

bochnia.description: Bochnia is one of Poland’s oldest cities, known for its historic salt mine and medieval heritage.

blocks.title: Bochnia Salt Mine
blocks.text: One of the oldest salt mines in Europe, offering underground tours and exhibitions.

blocks.title: Historic Center
blocks.text: Medieval streets and churches reflect the city’s long history.

villages.description: Małopolska’s villages preserve wooden houses, folk traditions, and peaceful landscapes.

blocks.title: Lanckorona
blocks.text: A charming hilltop village with wooden houses and artistic atmosphere.

blocks.title: Chochołów
blocks.text: A village of traditional wooden houses, preserved for generations.

blocks.title: Lipnica Murowana
blocks.text: Known for its wooden churches and cultural heritage.

landscapes.description: Małopolska offers dramatic peaks, rivers, forests, and valleys — from the Tatras to the Pieniny and Beskids.

blocks.title: Tatra Mountains
blocks.text: Poland’s highest peaks, with alpine scenery and iconic trails.

blocks.title: Pieniny & Dunajec Gorge
blocks.text: A spectacular river canyon, famous for rafting and scenic views.

blocks.title: Beskids
blocks.text: Gentle mountains ideal for hiking, cycling, and family trips.

food-and-beverages.description: Małopolska’s cuisine blends mountain flavors, traditional recipes, and regional specialties.

blocks.title: Oscypek
blocks.text: A smoked cheese made from sheep’s milk, a symbol of highlander culture.

blocks.title: Pierogi
blocks.text: Poland’s iconic dumplings, filled with cheese, potatoes, meat, or seasonal ingredients.

blocks.title: Kwaśnica
blocks.text: A hearty mountain soup made with sauerkraut and meat.

products.description: Local crafts, wooden carvings, lacework, pottery, and regional specialties from mountain villages.

blocks.title: Wooden Crafts
blocks.text: Hand‑carved decorations, furniture, and traditional ornaments.

blocks.title: Lacework & Textiles
blocks.text: Delicate handmade lace and woven fabrics from local artisans.

blocks.title: Pottery
blocks.text: Traditional ceramics shaped and painted by regional artists.

art.description: Małopolska’s art spans sacred architecture, folk traditions, and centuries of craftsmanship.

blocks.title: Wooden Churches
blocks.text: UNESCO‑listed wooden churches represent unique architectural heritage.

blocks.title: Folk Costumes
blocks.text: Colorful regional outfits reflect local identity and tradition.

blocks.title: Sacred Art
blocks.text: Altarpieces, sculptures, and paintings preserved in churches and museums.

unesco-sites.description: Małopolska hosts several UNESCO World Heritage sites, each with exceptional cultural value.

blocks.title: Kraków Old Town
blocks.text: A historic center filled with monuments, churches, and centuries of heritage.

blocks.title: Wieliczka Salt Mine
blocks.text: An underground world of chambers, sculptures, and saline lakes.

blocks.title: Wooden Churches
blocks.text: A unique collection of wooden sacral architecture.

craft.description: Craftsmanship in Małopolska reflects mountain traditions, woodwork, textiles, and artisan techniques.

blocks.title: Woodwork
blocks.text: Traditional carving techniques passed down through generations.

blocks.title: Textiles
blocks.text: Hand‑woven fabrics and embroidered garments.

blocks.title: Artisan Workshops
blocks.text: Local workshops preserve traditional skills and create modern interpretations.

businesses.description: Małopolska’s economy blends tourism, technology, education, and local enterprises.

blocks.title: Tourism
blocks.text: Cities, mountains, and cultural sites attract millions of visitors.

blocks.title: Innovation
blocks.text: Universities and tech companies drive research and development.

blocks.title: Local Enterprises
blocks.text: Small and medium businesses form the backbone of the regional economy.

treviso.description: Treviso is an intimate city of canals, arcades, and quiet piazzas. Its historic center blends medieval walls, Renaissance palaces, and the gentle flow of waterways that give it a unique, almost secret charm.

blocks.title: City of Water and Arcades
blocks.text: Treviso’s historic center is crossed by canals and lined with elegant arcades, where everyday life unfolds between cafés, small shops, and historic façades.

blocks.title: Piazzas and Daily Life
blocks.text: Piazza dei Signori and the surrounding streets are the beating heart of the city, where locals meet for coffee, aperitivo, and evening walks.

blocks.title: Flavors of the Territory
blocks.text: From Prosecco hills to local trattorias, Treviso is a gateway to authentic Venetian cuisine and wines.

padua.description: Padua is one of Italy’s oldest university cities, where science, faith, and art have intertwined for centuries. Its streets and squares tell stories of students, pilgrims, and merchants.

blocks.title: Ancient University Tradition
blocks.text: The University of Padua, founded in 1222, has hosted great minds such as Galileo Galilei and remains a vibrant center of research and culture.

blocks.title: Sacred Art and Pilgrimage
blocks.text: The Basilica of Saint Anthony and the Scrovegni Chapel with Giotto’s frescoes make Padua a key destination for art and spirituality.

blocks.title: Squares and Markets
blocks.text: Piazza delle Erbe and Piazza della Frutta host historic markets, where colors, aromas, and voices create a timeless atmosphere.

vicenza.description: Vicenza is the city of Palladio, where architecture becomes a living stage. Villas, palaces, and the iconic Teatro Olimpico make it a masterpiece of Renaissance harmony.

blocks.title: Palladian Heritage
blocks.text: Andrea Palladio’s villas and palaces have shaped Vicenza’s identity and influenced architecture across Europe and the world.

blocks.title: Teatro Olimpico
blocks.text: The Teatro Olimpico, with its extraordinary perspective stage, is one of the most fascinating theaters ever built.

blocks.title: Elegant City Life
blocks.text: Strolling through Corso Palladio and the historic center reveals boutiques, cafés, and a refined urban atmosphere.

belluno.description: Belluno is the gateway to the Dolomites, where mountains, forests, and valleys create a landscape of rare beauty. It is a city suspended between nature and history.

blocks.title: Door to the Dolomites
blocks.text: From Belluno, the Dolomites rise in all their majesty, offering trails, viewpoints, and unforgettable panoramas.

blocks.title: Nature and Outdoor Life
blocks.text: Hiking, skiing, cycling, and mountain refuges make the area a paradise for outdoor enthusiasts.

blocks.title: Historic Center
blocks.text: The historic center of Belluno preserves elegant palaces, churches, and squares with views over the surrounding mountains.

rovigo.description: Rovigo lies between the Po and Adige rivers, in the quiet landscape of Polesine. It is a land of water, horizons, and slow rhythms.

blocks.title: Between Rivers and Land
blocks.text: The territory around Rovigo is shaped by rivers, canals, and reclaimed lands, creating a unique landscape.

blocks.title: Medieval Traces
blocks.text: Towers, churches, and historic buildings tell the story of a city that has long been a crossroads between regions.

blocks.title: Quiet Atmosphere
blocks.text: Rovigo offers a calm, authentic rhythm of life, far from the most crowded tourist routes.

villages.description: The villages of Veneto are small worlds of stone, walls, and squares. Each one preserves stories, traditions, and views that seem painted.

blocks.title: Asolo
blocks.text: Known as the 'city of a hundred horizons', Asolo enchants with its views, arcades, and literary history.

blocks.title: Marostica
blocks.text: Famous for its living chess game, Marostica combines medieval walls, a castle, and a scenic square.

blocks.title: Bassano del Grappa
blocks.text: The wooden bridge over the Brenta river, grappa distilleries, and mountain views make Bassano unforgettable.

landscapes.description: From the Dolomites to Lake Garda and the Adriatic coast, Veneto offers a mosaic of landscapes: mountains, lakes, hills, plains, and sea.

blocks.title: Dolomites
blocks.text: A UNESCO World Heritage site, the Dolomites are a realm of rock towers, valleys, and changing light.

blocks.title: Lake Garda
blocks.text: The eastern shore of Lake Garda offers mild climate, olive trees, and lakeside villages.

blocks.title: Adriatic Coast
blocks.text: Beaches, lagoons, and fishing traditions define the coastal side of Veneto.

food-and-beverages.description: Veneto’s gastronomy is a journey through flavors: from mountain cheeses to lagoon fish, from traditional recipes to contemporary cuisine.

blocks.title: Radicchio Rosso di Treviso
blocks.text: A unique bitter‑sweet vegetable, cultivated with care and celebrated in winter dishes, salads, and risottos.

blocks.title: Soppressa Veneta
blocks.text: A traditional salami from the countryside, rich in flavor and often enjoyed with bread, polenta, and local wines.

blocks.title: Asiago DOP
blocks.text: A cheese from the Asiago plateau, ranging from fresh and delicate to aged and intense, perfect on its own or in recipes.

products.description: Veneto’s products reflect its diversity: from agricultural excellence to artisanal creations and design.

blocks.title: Local Specialties
blocks.text: Cheeses, cured meats, rice, polenta, and wines form the backbone of Veneto’s food culture.

blocks.title: Craft and Design
blocks.text: From glass and ceramics to textiles and furniture, Veneto is a land of craftsmanship and innovation.

blocks.title: Territory and Identity
blocks.text: Each product tells a story of land, climate, and human skill, connecting tradition and modernity.

art.description: Art in Veneto spans centuries: from Romanesque churches to Renaissance masterpieces, from Palladian villas to contemporary creativity.

blocks.title: Renaissance and Sacred Art
blocks.text: Churches, chapels, and palaces preserve frescoes, altarpieces, and sculptures that shaped European art.

blocks.title: Villas and Palaces
blocks.text: Noble residences in the countryside and cities tell stories of power, taste, and patronage.

blocks.title: Living Culture
blocks.text: Festivals, exhibitions, and cultural events keep the artistic tradition alive and evolving.

unesco-sites.description: Veneto hosts several UNESCO World Heritage sites, where nature, architecture, and history reach exceptional levels of value.

blocks.title: Venice and its Lagoon
blocks.text: A unique city on water, with canals, palaces, and a fragile yet extraordinary ecosystem.

blocks.title: Padua’s Fresco Cycles
blocks.text: The frescoes in Padua, including Giotto’s work, represent a milestone in European painting.

blocks.title: Dolomites
blocks.text: Recognized for their geological and scenic value, the Dolomites are a natural monument.

craft.description: Craftsmanship in Veneto is a blend of tradition and experimentation: glass, ceramics, wood, textiles, and more.

blocks.title: Glassmaking
blocks.text: Venetian glass is renowned worldwide for its colors, shapes, and techniques refined over centuries.

blocks.title: Wood and Furniture
blocks.text: From mountain workshops to design companies, woodcraft is a key part of the region’s identity.

blocks.title: Textiles and Fashion
blocks.text: Textile production and fashion design connect local know‑how with global markets.

businesses.description: Veneto’s economy is dynamic and diversified: industry, agriculture, tourism, services, and creative sectors coexist and interact.

blocks.title: Industrial Districts
blocks.text: Small and medium‑sized enterprises form productive clusters known for specialization and export capacity.

blocks.title: Agriculture and Wine
blocks.text: Vineyards, orchards, and fields produce wines, fruits, and products appreciated worldwide.

blocks.title: Tourism and Services
blocks.text: Cities of art, mountains, lakes, and coastlines support a strong tourism and service sector.
